senses.gloss: Jacob, Hebrew patriarch also known as Israel
James, any of a number of figures in the New Testament
a male given name, equivalent to English Jacob, James, or Jack or French Jacques

etymology.text: From Classical Syriac ܝܲܥܩܘܿܒ݂ (yaʿqōḇ) or Biblical Hebrew יַעֲקֹב (yaʿaqōv). Corresponding to Arabic يَعْقُبُ (yaʕqubu, “he follows”).
